fields.description:
# ACLI subprocess 429 handling: honor Retry-After if available, else jittered backoff

## Context / corrected premise
All ACLI calls go through `_run_acli` (`acli_subprocess.py`), which retries ALL non-zero exit codes (except
401/assignee) with a uniform 2s/4s `time.sleep(2**(attempt+1))`. The application-layer `_call_with_retry`
(`dispatch_one.py:100-144`) adds 429/5xx classification with delays [1,2,4]. NOTE: `_call_with_backoff`
(`acli_subprocess.py:236-292`) — a full exp-backoff+jitter+Retry-After wrapper — is DEAD CODE (zero production
call sites; the decoy from closed bug baldheaded-continuous-furseal); do NOT treat it as the live path. Also:
ACLI is a subprocess, so a Jira `Retry-After` header is not directly a Python object — availability is UNVERIFIED.

## What / files (`acli_subprocess.py`, `dispatch_one.py`)
1. PROBE FIRST (spike): run `acli jira workitem search` against a rate-limited/staged endpoint (needs
   JIRA_URL/JIRA_USER/JIRA_API_TOKEN) and record (a) whether ACLI surfaces `Retry-After` in parseable stderr,
   (b) the EXIT CODE ACLI returns on HTTP 429, and (c) whether the ACLI Go binary already retries 429 internally
   (so Python may never see it). Record findings on this ticket.
2. Implement 429 handling in the LIVE path (`_run_acli` retry loop and/or `_call_with_retry`) — either revive the
   dead `_call_with_backoff` logic INTO the live path or delete `_call_with_backoff` and inline equivalent
   jittered exp-backoff. Honor `Retry-After` IFF the probe confirmed availability (regex `Retry-After:\s*(\d+)`
   from stderr); else jittered exp-backoff without it (documented). Define a MAX retry count + total-backoff ceiling
   (e.g. 3 retries, 60s cap) so continuous 429s cannot hang the subprocess indefinitely.
3. Retryable-code change EXPLICIT: today all non-zero codes retry uniformly. Decide+document ADD-ON (keep existing,
   layer 429/502/503 backoff on top — recommended) vs NARROW to {429,502,503} (changes 502/503 + all-other-codes
   behavior; requires a test). Enumerate call sites affected: `acli_cli_ops.py` (6), `acli.py` (3).
4. PRESERVE the non-idempotent-write guard (`AcliTimeoutError` is not a `TimeoutError`).
5. Observability: log rate-limit detection, backoff trigger, and Retry-After honored-vs-unavailable.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Probe recorded: Retry-After availability, the 429 exit code, and whether ACLI retries 429 internally.
- [ ] 429 handled in the LIVE path (not the dead `_call_with_backoff`): jittered exp-backoff with a max-retry/ceiling; honors Retry-After IFF probe-confirmed. Proving command: `python -m pytest tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_error_taxonomy.py -q`.
- [ ] Retryable-code decision explicit+documented (add-on vs narrow); if narrowed, a test covers changed 502/503/other behavior; the existing non-429 retry behavior has an AC/test either way.
- [ ] No-blind-retry-on-timeout guard preserved; add a case to the EXISTING `tests/.../test_acli_subprocess_timeout.py` (per step 4 — no invented test name).
- [ ] Rate-limit/backoff/Retry-After events logged; decision captured in an ADR/design note.
